entities.name: WATERSHED INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Actions and practices implemented at the watershed level to manage natural resources

entities.name: NATURAL RESOURCE MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The practice of managing land, water, soil, plants, and animals to ensure the sustainable use of natural resources

entities.name: VEGETATIVE COVER
entities.description: The presence and extent of vegetation covering the land surface

entities.name: SEDIMENT MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The process of controlling and reducing soil erosion and sediment transport

entities.name: ABA GERIMA
entities.description: The specific target site in Ethiopia where watershed interventions were assessed

entities.name: ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The country where the case study was conducted

entities.name: LAND-USE LAND-COVER CHANGE
entities.description: The transformation of the natural landscape and the way land is utilized by humans

entities.name: CROPLAND
entities.description: Land used for the purpose of growing agricultural crops

entities.name: GRASSLAND
entities.description: Land dominated by grasses and other non-woody plants

entities.name: SHRUBLAND
entities.description: Land dominated by shrubs

entities.name: TEMPORAL SATELLITE IMAGERY
entities.description: The use of satellite images captured over time to analyze spatial and temporal changes

entities.name: GROUND MONITORING
entities.description: The process of collecting data through direct on-site measurements and observations

entities.name: SEDIMENT ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The buildup of soil and organic matter transported by water or wind

entities.name: FODDER BIOMASS
entities.description: The total mass of vegetation available for livestock feed

entities.name: VEGETATIVE COVER INTENSITY
entities.description: The density and vigor of the vegetation covering an area

entities.name: DEGRADED AND BARREN LAND
entities.description: Land that has lost its biological or economic productivity and lacks vegetation

entities.name: RESTORED VEGETATION
entities.description: Vegetation that has been re-established on previously degraded land

entities.name: PERENNIAL VEGETATION
entities.description: Plants that live for more than two years

entities.name: SEDIMENT RETENTION
entities.description: The capacity of a landscape to trap and hold sediment

entities.name: FODDER PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The rate at which fodder biomass is produced per unit area

entities.name: VEGETATION INTENSITY
entities.description: The degree of vegetation density in a given area

entities.name: SOIL AND VEGETATION ECOSYSTEM FUNCTIONS
entities.description: The biological, chemical, and physical processes that sustain the soil and plant ecosystem

entities.name: SOIL AND WATER ENVIRONMENTAL SERVICES
entities.description: The benefits provided by soil and water systems to humans and the environment

entities.name: COMMUNITY MANAGED LAND-USE REGULATIONS
entities.description: Local rules and guidelines established by the community to manage land use

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE LAND MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The use of land resources for meeting human needs while ensuring the long-term sustainability of the ecosystem
